response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, seasoned engineer with a genuine developer-tooling streak — he built C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI (an actual IDE), authored test frameworks and a test runner, and works on MCP code generators today. However, the role's core requirements are largely unmet: his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no Kotlin exposure, no Spring or enterprise-framework experience, and only peripheral Java work over a decade ago. Deep JVM understanding, the non-negotiable spine of this position, is not evidenced anywhere in the resume.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI, plus sustained language-tooling work (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Proven work on complex, large-scale systems, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing workshops, and long-lived open-source maintenance
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no evidence of deep JVM expertise, both listed as required qualifications
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise Java framework experience
Java appears only as a skills-list item with minor project use circa 2009-2011, far from the 'strong expertise' the role demands

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework background
